summary: The available literature indicates significant improvement in survival when combining therapies like radiofrequency ablation and chemotherapy versus monotherapy specifically among patients with nasopharyngeal carcinoma presenting only with lung metastasis. This supports the conclusion that combined modalities offer clinically meaningful benefits over single-modality approaches in this subset.
